messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agent asking for permission to write .tsx file within the WS ISSUE BODY: I asked for a small change to the file within the workspace, from the editor window that has that workspace open. also - there is no general dropdown to change permission to bypass/autopilot - the dropdown on "allow" in the permission widget does not open --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Permission System The SDK emits `permission.requested` events with a `kind` field. When `autopilot` / `autoApprove` permission level is set, all permissions are auto-approved without user interaction. Tool invocation messages are intentionally held in a queue (`toolCallWaitingForPermissions`) until the permission resolves, preventing a flash of "Running..." immediately followed by "Permission requested...". --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Permission Mode In the chat input area, you'll see the current permission mode (e.g., **"Edit automatically"**). Click it to change how Claude handles tool execution and file edits. Claude sessions have their own permission modes, distinct from other session targets: > **Note:** Before selecting Claude, you'll see the **Local** target's permission modes (e.g., "Default Approvals"). The modes below only appear after you switch to the Claude session target. | Mode | Label in UI | Behavior | |------|-------------|----------| | `default` | **Ask before edits** | Claude asks for confirmation before each edit. The safest mode — you approve every change. | | `acceptEdits` | **Edit automatically** | Claude auto-approves file edits within your workspace. Shell commands and other actions still require confirmation. This is the **default** for Claude sessions. | | `plan` | **Plan mode** | Claude first creates a plan describing what it intends to do, then asks you to approve before executing. | | `auto` | **Auto (model classifier)** | Uses a model classifier to automatically approve or deny tool operations. Visible by default; controlled by `github.copilot.chat.claudeAgent.allowAutoPermissions`. | | `bypassPermissions` | **Bypass all permissions** | All tools run without confirmation. ⚠️ Only visible when `github.copilot.chat.claudeAgent.allowDangerouslySkipPermissions` is enabled. **Recommended only for sandboxed environments with no internet access.** | You can change the permission mode at any time during a session by clicking the permission mode button at the bottom of the chat input. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Settings Reference | Setting | Type | Default | Description | |---------|------|---------|-------------| | `github.copilot.chat.claudeAgent.enabled` | boolean | `true` | Enable Claude Agent sessions in VS Code | | `github.copilot.chat.claudeAgent.allowDangerouslySkipPermissions` | boolean | `false` | Show "Bypass all permissions" option. ⚠️ Sandboxes only | | `github.copilot.chat.claudeAgent.allowAutoPermissions` | boolean | `true` | Show "Auto (model classifier)" permission mode option | --- --- File: openproject_full.md --- ## Possible errors and troubleshooting ### No permission to see this file If you are unable to see the details of a file or are unable to open some of the files linked to a work package, it could be related to your SharePoint account not having the necessary permissions. In such a case, you will be able to see the name of file, time of last modification and the name of the modifier but you will not be able to perform any further actions. To open or access these files, please contact your SharePoint administrator or the creator of the file so that they can grant you the necessary permissions. ![Error message based on missing permissions to see a file in OpenProject](openproject_sharepoint_no_permission_to_view.png) ===== D:\Antigravity\openproject\docs\user-guide\forums\README.md ===== --- sidebar_navigation: title: Forums priority: 820 description: Create and manage a forum in OpenProject keywords: forum, forums --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host system-prompt customization This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host** (ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's `extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix permission widget dropdown and ensure all modes are accessible",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "copilot", "permission-system"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code user using Claude Agent, I want the permission widget dropdown to open properly and all permission modes (Ask before edits, Edit automatically, Plan mode, Auto, Bypass all permissions) to be accessible so that I can quickly configure how the agent handles file modifications without unnecessary interruptions.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a Claude Agent session is active When hovering over or clicking the permission widget button Then the dropdown menu opens and displays all available permission modes",
    "Given the 'allowDangerouslySkipPermissions' setting is enabled When viewing the permission mode selector Then the 'Bypass all permissions' option should be visible in the dropdown",
    "Given a user clicks on any permission mode from the dropdown When the session starts or changes Then the agent respects and applies the selected permission mode for subsequent tool invocations",
    "Given the chat input area is focused When clicking the permission mode button at the bottom of the input Then the current mode can be changed without errors",
    "Given a file edit request is made While in 'Edit automatically' or 'Auto' mode Then the agent proceeds with the edit without showing a permission prompt"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Review permission event handling to ensure all modes are properly propagated",
      "Verify toolCallWaitingForPermissions queue logic respects selected mode",
      "Check if allowDangerouslySkipPermissions setting is correctly read and applied"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Fix the dropdown interaction bug in the permission widget - investigate why it doesn't open",
      "Ensure all permission modes are rendered in the dropdown menu",
      "Add visual feedback when a mode is selected (highlight/active state)",
      "Verify the global permission mode selector at chat input works correctly"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test dropdown opens and closes properly for each permission mode",
      "Verify 'Bypass all permissions' appears when allowDangerouslySkipPermissions is enabled",
      "Confirm agent respects selected modes during file edit requests",
      "Validate that shell commands still require confirmation regardless of edit mode"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Dropdown opens and displays all permission modes correctly",
    "All UI interactions tested and verified",
    "Backend permission propagation confirmed working",
    "Code reviewed by peer developer",
    "Unit tests passing for permission system",
    "E2E test covering mode selection and agent behavior"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Is this issue reproducible consistently across different VS Code versions?",
    "Are there any known workarounds users are currently using?",
    "Should we add a fallback mechanism if the dropdown fails to open?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The permission widget is part of the Copilot/Claude extension",
    "Users have basic VS Code permissions to interact with the UI",
    "The issue is primarily frontend-related (dropdown not opening)"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Permission System section",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Permission Mode table",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Settings Reference"
  ]
}
```
